history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 07 column D, facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to my right at row 07 column E is safe floor, so I should rotate clockwise to face that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor cell. The goal is not visible, and turning would not improve safety. Moving forward is the best safe exploration step while staying clear of lava and the wall on my right.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor cell. The goal is not visible, and turning would not immediately improve safety. Moving forward advances through the open corridor while avoiding lava and walls.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly ahead at row 06 column D is a safe floor tile. The cells to the sides are not immediately reachable without turning, and the wall column at E blocks movement to the right. Moving forward is safe and advances me through the open path.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is a safe floor tile. The cells to the left and right on my current row are lava and wall respectively, so moving forward is the only safe advance and may help route around the wall above to the right.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cells to my left and right on my current row are floor and wall respectively, so the safe option is to turn left to prepare to move onto the open floor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to the right, but I am currently facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, and moving forward will bring me closer to the opening through column D while avoiding the lava barrier in column E and the lava directly to my right. Advancing is the safest progress toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is blocked by lava. Both side-adjacent cells at my current row are also lava, so I should not move forward. I will turn right to reorient toward a safer route through the open column ahead-right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cells to my left and right on the current row are floor, but to reach either one I must first turn. I will turn left to face a safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front at row 06 column D is free floor, while the cells to the left and right on the current row are lava. The goal is visible ahead and to the right, and moving forward is the safe action that advances toward it.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to the right. I am facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor cell. The cells immediately to my left and right are lava, so turning would not help right now. Moving forward safely advances me toward the goal through the open column.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to the right at row 04 column F. I am at row 07 column D facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is safe floor. Moving forward brings me closer to the goal while avoiding the lava columns on both sides.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is empty floor, so moving forward is safe. The goal is visible ahead and to the right, and advancing through the open corridor is a good next step while avoiding the lava on both sides.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The goal is visible at row 06 column F, which is ahead and to the right. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is empty and safe, while turning is not needed yet. Moving forward advances me toward the goal without stepping on lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D, facing upward. The cells directly left and right of me on row 07 are lava at C and E, so moving sideways would be unsafe if I turned toward them. The goal is visible further to the right at F, but there is lava blocking that route along my current row. The cell directly ahead at row 06 column D is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Since forward is blocked and both immediate side cells are lava, I should turn to look for another safe direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with the cell directly in front at row 06 column D, which is lava. Moving forward would be deadly. The cells to my left and right on my current row are floor and wall respectively, so the safe immediate option is to turn left toward the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible directly to my left on the bottom row. The cell immediately in front of me is safe floor, but moving forward would not approach the goal. Since the goal is one cell to my left, I should first turn left to face it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly ahead of me: I am at row 07 column D facing upward, and the goal is at row 05 column D. However, the cell immediately in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to my right at row 07 column E is safe floor, so I should turn right to begin going around the lava toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible directly to my left on row 07 column B, while I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell in front of me is safe floor, but moving forward would take me away from the goal. The cell to my left is lava at row 07 column C, so I cannot reach the goal by stepping left immediately. A safer plan is to move forward into the open corridor and navigate around the lava barrier.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is free floor, so moving forward is safe. The goal is not visible, and turning would not help immediately. The cells to my sides include lava on the right, so the safest progress is to continue straight through the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is safe floor. The cells to my left and right contain lava, so moving forward is the only safe immediate move. Advancing also explores toward the open area above.
</thought>

MOVE_FORWARD

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor cell. </thought>
<action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a wall, so moving forward is blocked. Both left and right neighboring cells on my current row are open floor, but I must turn before moving. I will turn right to face a safe open cell.
</thought>
<action>
